mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a restrictive threshold (e.g., 4.5%), higher borrowing costs for corporations and consumers compress economic activity and future earnings, leading to negative equity returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve was in a tightening cycle from 2004-2006.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained momentum in nominal retail sales indicates strong consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in RSAFS has a rolling 3-year rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low unemployment rate and accelerating inflation signals an overheating economy, forcing the central bank into a more restrictive policy stance than anticipated, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Phillips Curve theory suggests a trade-off between inflation and unemployment.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects broad-based strength in the manufacturing and utility sectors, signaling robust corporate earnings and driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO fails to show a statistically significant positive correlation (p > 0.05) with 20-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year decline in housing starts, a key leading indicator, signals a significant contraction in construction and related consumer spending, leading to negative equity returns as recession fears mount.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market activity began to slow significantly in early 2006.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 does not experience a drawdown of at least 5% within 6 months in at least 2 out of 3 instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a robust and expanding economy, which supports higher corporate earnings expectations and positive very-short-term equity market performance.
failure_condition: The signal, defined as the monthly change in PAYEMS, shows a negative rank correlation with 5-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of decelerating real GDP growth and accelerating inflation creates a stagflationary environment, compressing corporate profit margins and reducing real consumer demand, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year GDPC1 growth < 2.5% AND year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth > 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Rising energy prices and slowing growth concerns were prevalent in 2006.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of quarters where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP is a strong signal of a potential recession, leading to sharp downward revisions in earnings forecasts and causing a significant decline in equity prices over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:NBER recession definition includes significant declines in economic activity.
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days following a GDP report showing contraction, the S&P 500 forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.
